10_PCA_biplot_cell_lines

Andrea Polazzi

2026-03-23

Setup

library(tidyverse)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'tibble' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'tidyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'readr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'purrr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'lubridate' was built under R version 4.5.2

## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(readxl)
source('../scripts/useful_plots.R')
data <- read_xlsx('../data/raw/tab1.xlsx')

Motifs subset

We keep only a subset of variables, corresponding to the ones we have available for primary tumors.

pca_data <- data %>% 
  select(`ALT assay strength`, TelFusDetector_rate, TelFuse_newtelomere, TelSeq, TERT_exp, ATAGGG, CTAGGG, GTAGGG, TAAGGG, TCAGGG, TGAGGG, TTCGGG, TTTGGG) %>%
  drop_na() %>% 
  rename(alt_status=`ALT assay strength`, tf_rate = TelFusDetector_rate, tel_insertion_rate = TelFuse_newtelomere, tel_content = TelSeq) %>% 
  mutate(alt_status = as.factor(alt_status))

We retain 83 observations (63 experimentally confirmed as non-ALT, 20 as ALT) but we don’t have the TTGGGG column.

pcaplt <- pca_plot(pca_data, title = 'PCA (motif subset)')
pcaplt$plot

biplot <- pca_biplot(pca_data, title = 'PCA biplot (motif subset)', scale_arrows = 5)
## Warning: package 'ggrepel' was built under R version 4.5.2
biplot$plot

Repeat using TVR column instead of individual motifs

pca_data <- data %>% 
  select(`ALT assay strength`, TelFusDetector_rate, TelFuse_newtelomere, TelSeq, TVR, TERT_exp) %>%
  drop_na() %>% 
  rename(alt_status=`ALT assay strength`, tf_rate = TelFusDetector_rate, tel_insertion_rate = TelFuse_newtelomere, tel_content = TelSeq) %>% 
  mutate(alt_status = as.factor(alt_status))
pcaplt <- pca_plot(pca_data, title = 'PCA plot (TVR feature)')
pcaplt$plot

biplot <- pca_biplot(pca_data, title = 'PCA biplot (TVR feature)', scale_arrows = 5)
biplot$plot

This is an almost exact replica of the paper’s Fig 1B.

Decompose TVR feature in individual motifs

pca_data <- data %>% 
  select(`ALT assay strength`, TelFusDetector_rate, TelFuse_newtelomere, TelSeq, 6:54, TERT_exp) %>%
  drop_na() %>% 
  rename(alt_status=`ALT assay strength`, tf_rate = TelFusDetector_rate, tel_insertion_rate = TelFuse_newtelomere, tel_content = TelSeq) %>% 
  mutate(alt_status = as.factor(alt_status))
pcaplt <- pca_plot(pca_data, title = 'PCA (all motifs)')
pcaplt$plot

biplot <- pca_biplot(pca_data, title = 'PCA biplot (all motifs)', scale_arrows = 30)
biplot$plot

Interpretation and discussion

In the PCAWG primary tumor dataset, the biplot revealed a clear separation of ALT-high tumors along PC1 (53.1%), driven primarily by tel_insertion_rate, tf_rate, and tel_content. Notably, specific TVR motifs, in particular TTCGGG and TGAGGG, loaded in the direction of ALT-high samples, while TTTGGG loaded in the opposite direction. These results are consistent with published literature and confirmed by supervised classification results.

In the cell line dataset (Bennett et al., 2026), replicating the authors’ Figure 1B using the pre-computed batch-corrected TVR feature confirmed that TVR content, together with neo-telomere structures and telomere fusion rate, are the strongest contributors to ALT-positive separation along PC1 (67.5%), while TERT expression loads in the opposite direction. This is fully consistent with the original paper and with the PCAWG results.

Two additional exploratory biplots were generated using raw individual motif frequencies from the cell line dataset. In both cases, the motif vectors pointed toward ALT-negative samples, opposite to what was observed in the PCAWG dataset. In PCAWG, motif values are per-sample normalized z-scores (singleton distances), whereas in the cell line dataset they are raw frequencies subject to sequencing depth variation and batch effects. Without applying the batch-correction pipeline used by Bennett et al. (log-transformation followed by regression on depth-derived principal components) individual motif frequencies are not directly interpretable in the context of ALT biology, and direct comparison with the PCAWG results is not valid.

Restrict to primary tumors only

Importantly, I noticed that the training set they used, and on which I replicated their PCA, contained both primary AND metastatic tumors. We could see if, retaining only primary tumors, the same feature contributions to PCA emerge.

pca_data_primary <- data %>% 
  filter(PrimaryOrMetastasis == 'Primary') %>% 
  select(`ALT assay strength`, TelFusDetector_rate, TelFuse_newtelomere, TelSeq, TERT_exp, ATAGGG, CTAGGG, GTAGGG, TAAGGG, TCAGGG, TGAGGG, TTCGGG, TTTGGG) %>%
  drop_na() %>% 
  rename(alt_status=`ALT assay strength`, tf_rate = TelFusDetector_rate, tel_insertion_rate = TelFuse_newtelomere, tel_content = TelSeq) %>% 
  mutate(alt_status = as.factor(alt_status))

We have only 50 observations (38 non-ALT, 12 ALT).

biplot <- pca_biplot(pca_data_primary, title = 'PCA biplot on primary tumors (motif subset)')
biplot$plot

pca_data_primary <- data %>% 
  filter(PrimaryOrMetastasis=='Primary') %>% 
  select(`ALT assay strength`, TelFusDetector_rate, TelFuse_newtelomere, TelSeq, TVR, TERT_exp) %>%
  drop_na() %>% 
  rename(alt_status=`ALT assay strength`, tf_rate = TelFusDetector_rate, tel_insertion_rate = TelFuse_newtelomere, tel_content = TelSeq) %>% 
  mutate(alt_status = as.factor(alt_status))

biplot <- pca_biplot(pca_data_primary, title = 'PCA biplot on primary tumors (TVR feature)', scale_arrows = 5)
biplot$plot

pca_data_primary <- data %>% 
  filter(PrimaryOrMetastasis=='Primary') %>% 
  select(`ALT assay strength`, TelFusDetector_rate, TelFuse_newtelomere, TelSeq, 6:54, TERT_exp) %>%
  drop_na() %>% 
  rename(alt_status=`ALT assay strength`, tf_rate = TelFusDetector_rate, tel_insertion_rate = TelFuse_newtelomere, tel_content = TelSeq) %>% 
  mutate(alt_status = as.factor(alt_status))

We have even fewer observations (20 non-ALT, 12 ALT), since many rows have NA values in individual motifs columns.

biplot <- pca_biplot(pca_data_primary, title = 'PCA biplot on primary tumors (all motifs)', scale_arrows = 30)
biplot$plot

By looking at the resulting PCA biplots, we can see how the contributions of features in the PCA is very much the same as before, even when restricting the analysis only for primary tumor cell lines.

Using a larger dataset

We will now re-do the analysis, enlarging the dataset to more observations, coming from outside the training set. In particular, we will use observations for which no ALT assay strength value is present, but having a predicted value in ALT_pred_class with a strong enough ALT_pred_prob.
